mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the latest CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your requirements.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --namespace=default

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/upgrade.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configurations.
> This command applies the upgrade configuration.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is private.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image exists and is accessible, or authenticate to the container registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get services' and ensure the deployment is healthy.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for legacy scheduling methods.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
